briefs.title: Vancouver scraps 2022 city plan; 50+ land use rules rewritten effective March 31, 2026
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has cancelled the 2022 Vancouver Plan and replaced it with a new Vancouver Official Development Plan (the city's master rulebook for how land can be used and built on). As a result, more than 50 area plans, rezoning policies, and land use documents have been updated, with all changes taking effect March 31, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Review how the new Vancouver Official Development Plan affects your property's zoning or any planned development before March 31, 2026.

key_dates.label: Vancouver Official Development Plan and 50+ land use policy amendments take effect

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Real Estate

briefs.title: 250-Room Floating Hotel at Canada Place Moves Toward Public Hearing
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has referred a zoning change application for 1055–1085 Canada Place to a Public Hearing (a formal meeting where the public can speak before a vote). The proposal would allow a 250-room floating hotel at that location, with higher floor area and a building height limit of 20 metres.
briefs.action_required: Attend or submit comments at the upcoming Public Hearing if this development affects your business.

briefs.title: Brewery lounges get two more years of relaxed floor area rules
briefs.summary: City council has extended a temporary suspension of zoning rules that limit how much floor space a brewery can use for its lounge area. This suspension — first put in place to give breweries more flexibility — now runs for two additional years, meaning breweries can continue operating larger lounge spaces without needing a special exemption.
briefs.action_required: Check your brewery lounge floor area to confirm you are operating within the now-extended permissions.

briefs.title: Pennyfarthing Properties front-ender agreement raised to $1,211,720 over 10 years
briefs.summary: City council approved a change to a cost-sharing agreement (a deal where a developer pays upfront for public utilities and gets repaid later) with Pennyfarthing Properties Ash St. North Ltd. The maximum repayment amount has been raised to $1,211,720, to be paid back from a Development Cost Levy reserve fund over 10 years.

briefs.title: $2.36M Federal Grant Approved for Champlain Heights Community Centre Energy Retrofit
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has endorsed a $2,359,000 grant application to the federal Green and Inclusive Community Buildings program to fund an energy retrofit (upgrades that reduce energy use and emissions) at the Champlain Heights Community Centre. The city also approved adjustments to its capital plan to support the project.

briefs.title: Vancouver approves $1.4M in grants to upgrade Downtown Eastside rooming houses
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council approved three SRO (single-room occupancy — low-cost rooming house) upgrading grants totalling $899,753 and one cooling/warming retrofit grant of $500,000. Both grants go to the Downtown Eastside SRO Collaborative Society and are funded by revenue collected from the Empty Homes Tax.

briefs.title: Vancouver launches consultation on Villages Planning Program zoning amendments
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has directed staff to consult with school boards, First Nations, TransLink, BC Hydro, and business improvement associations on proposed changes to the ODP (Official Development Plan — the city's master zoning rulebook) for the Villages Planning Program. These amendments could reshape how land is used and developed in village-style commercial and mixed-use areas across Vancouver.
briefs.action_required: Contact your local business improvement association to find out how to participate in the consultation.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Vancouver ODP rezoning amendments enter public consultation — affected property owners should engage now
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has directed staff to consult with affected people, businesses, and organizations about proposed changes to the Vancouver Official Development Plan (ODP) — the city's master document that controls how land can be used and developed. The changes apply to rezoning applications that are already in progress (called 'in-stream' applications), meaning projects currently seeking approval to change how a property is zoned.
briefs.action_required: If your property or project is listed in Appendix A, contact Vancouver City staff to participate in the consultation.

briefs.title: Vancouver repeals old greenhouse gas bylaw after adopting new city-wide development plan
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has cancelled (repealed) its old Greenhouse Gas Emission Reduction Official Development Plan bylaw (By-law No. 10041). This was done as a direct result of the city adopting a new Official Development Plan (ODP) — the master document that guides how land in Vancouver can be used and built on.

briefs.title: Vancouver repeals old Regional Context Statement planning bylaw after adopting new city-wide development plan
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has cancelled (repealed) the old Regional Context Statement Official Development Plan bylaw (By-law No. 10789). This was a required housekeeping step after the city adopted its new Official Development Plan (ODP) — the master document that guides how and where the city grows and develops.

briefs.title: Vancouver adopts new city-wide land use plan, reshaping where and what you can build
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has passed By-law No. 14660, officially adopting the Vancouver Official Development Plan — the city's new master document that sets the rules for how land across Vancouver can be used and developed. This plan guides decisions on zoning (what types of buildings or businesses are allowed in each area), density, and future growth, and will influence permits and development approvals going forward.
briefs.action_required: Review the Vancouver Official Development Plan to see how new land use rules may affect your property or business location.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Vancouver Subdivision By-law amended for 1110–1160 West King Edward Avenue site
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed By-law No. 14659, which changes the Subdivision By-law (the rules that govern how land can be divided into separate parcels) for the property at 1110–1160 West King Edward Avenue. This affects how that specific site can be subdivided or developed going forward.
briefs.action_required: If you own or are developing 1110–1160 West King Edward Ave, review By-law No. 14659 with your planner.

briefs.title: Noise Control By-law Now Covers Prior Street, Malkin Avenue, and Station Street Properties
briefs.summary: Vancouver has updated its Noise Control By-law (By-law No. 14658) to include a specific development zone covering 450–496 Prior Street, 550 Malkin Avenue, and 1002 Station Street. Businesses or property owners in this area must now follow the city's noise rules that apply to this district.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business operates at these addresses and review Vancouver's Noise Control By-law for compliance.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Sign rules updated for three Vancouver addresses in CD-1 zone
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed By-law No. 14657, which sets new sign rules for properties at 450–496 Prior Street, 550 Malkin Avenue, and 1002 Station Street. These rules apply to the CD-1 zone (a custom zoning district with site-specific development rules) and control what types, sizes, and placements of signs are allowed at these locations.
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease space at these addresses, review the new sign rules before installing or changing any signage.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Noise Control By-law Now Covers 2096 West Broadway and Arbutus Street Development Site
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed By-law No. 14656, which adds the CD-1 zoning district (a custom development zone approved for a specific site) at 2096 West Broadway and 2560–2576 Arbutus Street to the city's Noise Control By-law. Businesses and contractors operating at or near this site must now follow Vancouver's noise rules.
briefs.action_required: Check Vancouver's Noise Control By-law rules if you operate or do construction at 2096 West Broadway or Arbutus Street.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Sign rules updated for West Broadway and Arbutus Street mixed-use development site
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed By-law No. 14655, which sets new sign rules for the CD-1 zone (a custom zoning district with its own specific rules) at 2096 West Broadway and 2560–2576 Arbutus Street. Businesses or developers operating at these addresses must now follow these updated sign regulations.
briefs.action_required: Check the new sign rules for your address before installing or changing any signage at this site.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Noise rules now apply to 800 Commercial Drive under updated Vancouver by-law
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed By-law No. 14654, which adds the CD-1 zoning district (a custom zoning area with its own specific rules) at 800 Commercial Drive to the city's Noise Control By-law. Businesses and tenants at that address must now follow the same noise limits and restrictions that apply to other regulated properties in Vancouver.
briefs.action_required: Check your noise levels and hours of operation against Vancouver's Noise Control By-law if you operate at 800 Commercial Drive.

briefs.title: Sign rules updated for 800 Commercial Drive — what businesses there must know
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed By-law No. 14653, which sets new sign rules specifically for the CD-1 zoning district (a custom zoning area with its own tailored rules) at 800 Commercial Drive. Any business or developer at that address must now follow these updated sign regulations when installing, replacing, or modifying signage.
briefs.action_required: Check the new sign rules for 800 Commercial Drive CD-1 before installing or changing any signage.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Housing Agreement By-law Enacted for Granville Street and West 37th Avenue Development
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed By-law No. 14652, which creates a housing agreement (a legal contract registered on a property's title that locks in rules about how housing units must be used) for the development at 5238–5262 Granville Street and 1495 West 37th Avenue. This agreement sets binding conditions on how the residential units at that site can be rented or sold.
briefs.action_required: If you own or are developing property at this address, review By-law No. 14652 with your lawyer.

briefs.title: Vancouver housing agreement locked in for 6055 Fraser Street residential development
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed By-law No. 14651, creating a housing agreement (a legal contract registered on a property's title that sets rules about how housing units must be used or priced) for the residential development at 6055 Fraser Street. This agreement binds current and future owners of the property to specific housing conditions set by the City.
briefs.action_required: If you own or are purchasing 6055 Fraser Street, review By-law No. 14651 to understand the housing conditions tied to the property title.

briefs.title: Vancouver housing agreement registered for Dunbar Street residential development at 5889–5925
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed By-law No. 14650, which creates a housing agreement (a legal contract registered on a property's title that locks in rules about how the housing must be used) for the residential development at 5889–5925 Dunbar Street. This agreement sets binding conditions on that specific property and its future owners.

briefs.title: Fines updated for street, traffic, and solid waste rule violations
briefs.summary: The city passed By-law No. 14649, which updates the list of ticketable offences (rule-breaking that results in a fine) to match recent changes to the Street and Traffic By-law and the Solid Waste By-law. This means some street, traffic, and garbage-related violations may now carry new or revised fines.
briefs.action_required: Review your business practices around waste disposal and street/traffic use to ensure you comply with the updated rules.

briefs.title: Property owners now liable for refuse (garbage) left on public streets under amended bylaw
briefs.summary: A new bylaw amendment (By-law No. 14648) makes property owners and occupiers legally responsible if garbage or waste is deposited on a public street from their property. If refuse from your property ends up on the street, you can now be held liable (legally responsible and potentially fined) under the Street and Traffic By-law.
briefs.action_required: Check that garbage, recycling, and waste from your property is properly contained and never left on the street.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Construction & Development

briefs.title: 414–420 West Pender Street rezoned to CD-1 Comprehensive Development District
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed By-law No. 14647, changing the zoning (the official rules about how land can be used and what can be built) at 414–420 West Pender Street to CD-1 — a custom zoning category that sets site-specific rules for development. This means the permitted uses and building rules for that property have changed.
briefs.action_required: If you own, lease, or plan to operate at 414–420 West Pender, review the new CD-1 zoning rules with your lawyer or planner.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Heather Lands rezoning approved: 317,861 sq.m mixed-use development, 4–46 storeys
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has approved in principle a major rezoning of the Heather Lands (near Heather Street, West 35th and 37th Avenue) to allow a large, multi-phase mixed-use development totalling 317,861 square metres, with buildings ranging from 4 to 46 storeys. The approval includes new zoning rules (land-use regulations), housing agreements, and updates to sign and noise control bylaws.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near Heather Street and West 35th/37th Ave, review the new noise and sign bylaws for compliance requirements.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
